entry.title: docker教程
entry.content:
介绍

Docker 就像一个盒子，里面可以装很多物件，如果需要某些物件，可以直接将该盒子拿走，而不需要从该盒子中一件一件的取。

安装

windows安装

安装教程

概念

镜像(Image)

Docker的镜像概念类似于虚拟机里的镜像(比如.ISO文件)，是一个只读的模板，一个独立的文件系统，包括运行容器所需的数据，可以用来创建新的容器。

容器(Container)

Docker容器是由Docker镜像创建的运行实例，类似VM虚拟机，支持启动，停止，删除等。
每个容器间是相互隔离的，容器中会运行特定的应用，包含特定应用的代码及所需的依赖文件。

仓库(Repository)

镜像便于传播,而仓库就是专门用来传播这些镜像的地方,他有点类似与Github,或者你可以把他看成一个存放各种镜像的镜像商店

entry.title: leetcode 139.单词拆分
entry.content:
给你一个字符串 s 和一个字符串列表 wordDict 作为字典。如果可以利用字典中出现的一个或多个单词拼接出 s 则返回 true。

**注意：**不要求字典中出现的单词全部都使用，并且字典中的单词可以重复使用。

示例 1：

输入: s = "leetcode", wordDict = ["leet", "code"]
输出: true
解释: 返回 true 因为 "leetcode" 可以由 "leet" 和 "code" 拼接成。

示例 2：

输入: s = "applepenapple", wordDict = ["apple", "pen"]
输出: true
解释: 返回 true 因为 "applepenapple" 可以由 "apple" "pen" "apple" 拼接成。 注意，你可以重复使用字典中的单词。

示例 3：

输入: s = "catsandog", wordDict = ["cats", "dog", "sand", "and", "cat"]
输出: false

提示：

  • 1 <= s.length <= 300
  • 1 <= wordDict.length <= 1000
  • 1 <= wordDict[i].length <= 20
  • s 和 wordDict[i] 仅由小写英文字母组成
  • wordDict 中的所有字符串 互不相同

程序

import java.util.List;
import java.util.Set;
import java.util.HashSet;

class Solution {
    public boolean wordBreak(String s, List<String> wordDict) {
        int n = s.length();
        s = " " + s; //从1开始
        Set<Integer> hash = new HashSet<>(); //存入字符串hash值
        //计算字符串hash值
        final int P = 131;
        final int MOD = (int)1e9 + 7; //java中没有 unsigned long long 类型
        for(String word : wordDict){
            long h = 0;
            for(char c : word.toCharArray()){
                h = (h * P + c) % MOD;
            }
            hash.add((int)h);
        }
        //f[i]表示前i个字符是否可以被拆分
        boolean[] f = new boolean[n + 1];
        f[0] = true;
        //递推能转移到哪些状态
        for(int i = 0; i <= n; i++){
            long h = 0;
            if(!f[i]) continue;
            for(int j = i+1; j <= n; j++)
            {
                h = (h * P + s.charAt(j)) % MOD;
                if(hash.contains((int)h)){
                    f[j] = true;
                }
            }
        }
        return f[n];
    }
}

entry.title: AtCoder ABC330.E Mex and Update
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

You are given a sequence A=(A1,A2,…,AN)A=(A_1,A_2,\dots,A_N)A=(A1​,A2​,…,AN​) of length NNN.
Respond to the following QQQ queries in the order they are given.

The kkk-th query is given in the following format:

i_k
x_k
  • First, change AikA_{i_k}Aik​​ to xkx_kxk​. This change will carry over to subsequent queries.
  • Then, print the mex\rm{mex}mex of AAA.
    • The mex\rm{mex}mex of AAA is the smallest non-negative integer not contained in AAA.

Constraints

  • All input values are integers.
  • 1≤N,Q≤2×1051 \le N,Q \le 2 \times 10^51≤N,Q≤2×105
  • 0≤Ai≤1090 \le A_i \le 10^90≤Ai​≤109
  • 1≤ik≤N1 \le i_k \le N1≤ik​≤N
  • 0≤xk≤1090 \le x_k \le 10^90≤xk​≤109

entry.title: Atcoder ABC330.D Counting Ls
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

You are given an N×NN \times NN×N grid. Let (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) denote the cell in the iii-th row from the top and the jjj-th column from the left.
The states of the cells are given by NNN strings of length NNN, S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \dots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​, in the following format:

  • If the jjj-th character of SiS_iSi​ is o, there is an o written in cell (i,j)(i,j)(i,j).
  • If the jjj-th character of SiS_iSi​ is x, there is an x written in cell (i,j)(i,j)(i,j).

Find the number of triples of cells that satisfy all of the following conditions:

  • The three cells in the triple are distinct.
  • All three cells have an o written in them.
  • Exactly two of the cells are in the same row.
  • Exactly two of the cells are in the same column.

Here, two triples are considered different if and only if some cell is contained in exactly one of the triples.

Constraints

  • NNN is an integer between 222 and 200020002000, inclusive.
  • SiS_iSi​ is a string of length NNN consisting of o and x.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC330.C Minimize Abs 2
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

You are given a positive integer DDD.

Find the minimum value of ∣x2+y2−D∣|x^2+y^2-D|∣x2+y2−D∣ for non-negative integers xxx and yyy.

Constraints

  • 1≤D≤2×10121\leq D \leq 2\times 10^{12}1≤D≤2×1012
  • All input values are integers.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC346.E Paint
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

There is a grid with HHH rows and WWW columns. Initially, all cells are painted with color 000.

You will perform the following operations in the order i=1,2,…,Mi = 1, 2, \ldots, Mi=1,2,…,M.

  • If Ti=1T_i = 1Ti​=1, repaint all cells in the AiA_iAi​-th row with color XiX_iXi​.

  • If Ti=2T_i = 2Ti​=2, repaint all cells in the AiA_iAi​-th column with color XiX_iXi​.

After all operations are completed, for each color iii that exists on the grid, find the number of cells that are painted with color iii.

Constraints

  • 1≤H,W,M≤2×1051 \leq H, W, M \leq 2 \times 10^51≤H,W,M≤2×105
  • Ti∈{1,2}T_i \in \lbrace 1, 2 \rbraceTi​∈{1,2}
  • 1≤Ai≤H1 \leq A_i \leq H1≤Ai​≤H for each iii such that Ti=1T_i = 1Ti​=1,
  • 1≤Ai≤W1 \leq A_i \leq W1≤Ai​≤W for each iii such that Ti=2T_i = 2Ti​=2.
  • 0≤Xi≤2×1050 \leq X_i \leq 2 \times 10^50≤Xi​≤2×105
  • All input values are integers.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC346.D Gomamayo Sequence
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

You are given a string SSS of length NNN consisting of 0 and 1.

A string TTT of length NNN consisting of 0 and 1 is a good string if and only if it satisfies the following condition:

  • There is exactly one integer iii such that 1≤i≤N−11 \leq i \leq N - 11≤i≤N−1 and the iii-th and (i+1)(i + 1)(i+1)-th characters of TTT are the same.

For each i=1,2,…,Ni = 1,2,\ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N, you can choose whether or not to perform the following operation once:

  • If the iii-th character of SSS is 0, replace it with 1, and vice versa. The cost of this operation, if performed, is CiC_iCi​.

Find the minimum total cost required to make SSS a good string.

Constraints

  • 2≤N≤2×1052 \leq N \leq 2 \times 10^52≤N≤2×105
  • SSS is a string of length NNN consisting of 0 and 1.
  • 1≤Ci≤1091 \leq C_i \leq 10^91≤Ci​≤109
  • NNN and CiC_iCi​ are integers.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC346.C Σ
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

You are given a sequence of positive integers A=(A1,A2,…,AN)A=(A_1,A_2,\dots,A_N)A=(A1​,A2​,…,AN​) of length NNN and a positive integer KKK.

Find the sum of the integers between 111 and KKK, inclusive, that do not appear in the sequence AAA.

Constraints

  • 1≤N≤2×1051\leq N \leq 2\times 10^51≤N≤2×105
  • 1≤K≤2×1091\leq K \leq 2\times 10^91≤K≤2×109
  • 1≤Ai≤2×1091\leq A_i \leq 2\times 10^91≤Ai​≤2×109
  • All input values are integers.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC331.E Set Meal
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

AtCoder cafeteria sells meals consisting of a main dish and a side dish.
There are NNN types of main dishes, called main dish 111, main dish 222, …\dots…, main dish NNN. Main dish iii costs aia_iai​ yen.
There are MMM types of side dishes, called side dish 111, side dish 222, …\dots…, side dish MMM. Side dish iii costs bib_ibi​ yen.

A set meal is composed by choosing one main dish and one side dish. The price of a set meal is the sum of the prices of the chosen main dish and side dish.
However, for LLL distinct pairs (c1,d1),…,(cL,dL)(c_1, d_1), \dots, (c_L, d_L)(c1​,d1​),…,(cL​,dL​), the set meal consisting of main dish cic_ici​ and side dish did_idi​ is not offered because they do not go well together.
That is, NM−LNM - LNM−L set meals are offered. (The constraints guarantee that at least one set meal is offered.)

Find the price of the most expensive set meal offered.

Constraints

  • 1≤N,M≤1051 \leq N, M \leq 10^51≤N,M≤105
  • 0≤L≤min⁡(105,NM−1)0 \leq L \leq \min(10^5, NM - 1)0≤L≤min(105,NM−1)
  • 1≤ai,bi≤1091 \leq a_i, b_i \leq 10^91≤ai​,bi​≤109
  • 1≤ci≤N1 \leq c_i \leq N1≤ci​≤N
  • 1≤dj≤M1 \leq d_j \leq M1≤dj​≤M
  • (ci,di)≠(cj,dj)(c_i, d_i) \neq (c_j, d_j)(ci​,di​)​=(cj​,dj​) if i≠ji \neq ji​=j.
  • All input values are integers.

entry.title: AtCoder ABC331.D Tile Pattern
entry.content:
原题

Problem Statement

There is a grid with 10910^9109 by 10910^9109 squares. Let (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) denote the square at the (i+1)(i + 1)(i+1)-th row from the top and the (j+1)(j + 1)(j+1)-th column from the left (0≤i,j<109)(0 \leq i, j \lt 10^9)(0≤i,j<109). (Note the unusual index assignment.)
Each square is black or white. The color of the square (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is represented by a character P[i mod N][j mod N]P[i \bmod N][j \bmod N]P[imodN][jmodN], where B means black, and W means white. Here, a mod ba \bmod bamodb denotes the remainder when aaa is divided by bbb.

Answer QQQ queries.
Each query gives you four integers A,B,C,DA, B, C, DA,B,C,D and asks you to find the number of black squares contained in the rectangular area with (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) as the top-left corner and (C,D)(C, D)(C,D) as the bottom-right corner.

Constraints

  • 1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000
  • P[i][j]P[i][j]P[i][j] is W or B.
  • 1≤Q≤2×1051 \leq Q \leq 2 \times 10^51≤Q≤2×105
  • 0≤A≤C<1090 \leq A \leq C \lt 10^90≤A≤C<109
  • 0≤B≤D<1090 \leq B \leq D \lt 10^90≤B≤D<109
  • N,Q,A,B,C,DN, Q, A, B, C, DN,Q,A,B,C,D are all integers.
